validated_reasoning: The study explicitly discusses interpretability through the use of LDA as a topic-modelling technique, which is inherently interpretable. It provides insights into the underlying topics and assigns probabilities to each photo, making the model's outputs understandable and traceable. This is evidenced by the discussion of how LDA identifies topics and produces gamma values, which are directly interpretable outputs.

validated_bibliography.direct_quote: LDA is a topic-modelling technique that assumes the existence of a hidden structure consisting of a set of topics in the whole corpus of photos (Tirunillai & Tellis, 2014). It helps identify the underlying topics from a large number of photos by using the co-occurrence of labels in those photos.
The LDA model also produces gamma values -a probability distribution over topics -of each photo. Each photo was assigned to its most probable gaze for further analysis.
LDA modelling presented the dominant or representative labels within each gaze.
